channel.title: SevenAtSevenLastSection
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SevenAtSevenLastSection, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Reasons Why Women Experience Hair Thinning
Criminally In Love: Mystery & Crime & Romance In One
item.description: Amateur sleuths, professional investigators, and even contract killers dibble and dabble in these romantic mysteries and thrillers.

item.title: 100+ Black-Owned Bookstores in the U.S.
item.description: There are hundreds of Black-owned bookstores in the U.S. to explore! Check them out here, sorted by state.

item.title: New Nonfiction by Black Authors to Read in 2026
item.description: From a memoir by Jesmyn Ward and Kimberlé Crenshaw to a new release by Ibram X. Kendi, these are the best new Black nonfiction books to read in 2026.

item.title: How HIV Affects Older Adults Differently
Exercise Benefits for a Faster Metabolism
item.description: More intense workouts can temporarily raise your metabolic rate. Learn how exercise affects metabolism and which workouts have the greatest impact.

item.title: How Accurate Should Historical Fiction Be?
item.description: If you're a reader of historical fiction, or you want to write it yourself, read what the greats have to say about historical accuracy.

item.title: Minneapolis Comic & Bookstore Owner Greg Ketter Emerges as a Symbol of Protest Against ICE Actions
item.description: Graphic novel sales have risen 9%, LORE OLYMOUS is getting adapted, season 2 of ONE PIECE, and more.

item.title: 3 Ingredient Cool Whip Candy
item.description: This 3 Ingredient Cool Whip Candy tastes just like a Three Musketeers Bar! Fluffy on the inside, smooth chocolate on…

item.title: After Disputed Idaho Raid, Lawsuit Tries Different Approach to Hold ICE Accountable
item.description: It’s nearly impossible to sue federal officials for civil rights violations, but a new ACLU filing could become a test case for how to do it.

item.title: Investigated for a Positive Drug Test While Giving Birth? Tell Us What Happened Next.
item.description: Help our team continue their reporting on referrals to law enforcement for alleged drug use during pregnancy by sharing your story.

item.title: Tens of Thousands of Mothers Were Flagged to Police Over Flawed Drug Tests at Childbirth
item.description: The Marshall Project found more than 70,000 cases referred to law enforcement over allegations of substance use during pregnancy — and that’s a signif...

item.title: How We Counted Referrals to Police About Substance Use During Pregnancy
item.description: New analysis finds the parents of 70,000 newborns were reported to law enforcement over substance use allegations, often based on flawed drug tests.

item.title: Deadly Errors: Why People Keep Dying Inside Cuyahoga County’s Jail
item.description: Jail administrators fail to fix mistakes that continue to cost lives, an analysis of state records by The Marshall Project - Cleveland shows.
